clues.clue: This chain with golden arches was built into a giant by Ray Kroc from a brothers' stand.
This flame-grilled chain bills itself the 'Home of the Whopper.'
This square-patty chain was named for founder Dave Thomas's red-haired daughter.
This West Coast chain is famous for a secret menu and 'animal style' orders.
This Wisconsin-born chain is known for 'ButterBurgers' and frozen custard.
This red-haired, yellow-jumpsuited clown has fronted McDonald's ads for decades.
This white-suited, goateed 'Colonel' is the face of a fried chicken empire.
A talking Chihuahua in late-90s ads pined 'Yo quiero...' for this Mexican-style chain.
This chain's ad mascot is a calm executive topped with a giant round white ball for a head.
clues.response: What is Jack in the Box?

clues.clue: Toucan Sam, who follows his nose, fronts this fruity Kellogg's loop cereal sold at no drive-thru.
'I'm Lovin' It,' set to a famous five-note jingle, belongs to this arches chain.
'Eat Fresh' was long the tagline of this footlong-sandwich chain.
'Think Outside the Bun' urged customers toward this taco-and-burrito chain.
'Finger Lickin' Good' is the classic line of this Colonel's chicken chain.
'Have It Your Way' was for decades the motto of this Whopper-selling chain.
This McDonald's breakfast sandwich stacks egg, cheese and Canadian bacon on a muffin.
'Two all-beef patties, special sauce, lettuce, cheese' describes this towering McDonald's burger.
This thick Wendy's dessert comes in chocolate or vanilla and resists a straw.
Taco Bell's hexagonal grilled item is the 'Crunchwrap' followed by this word.
Arby's built its reputation on thin-sliced piles of this seasoned beef.
White Castle, opened in 1921, is often credited as the first chain selling these small square patties.
The McDonald brothers launched their original stand in San Bernardino, in this U.S. state.
Colonel Sanders first cooked his secret-recipe chicken at a roadside spot in this Bluegrass State.
A standard Subway 'footlong' sandwich measures this many inches.
Before franchising burgers, Ray Kroc sold these five-spindled milkshake machines.

final.clue: This chain once promised '30 minutes or it's free' and uses a red-and-blue domino tile logo.
